differential_diagnoses: Common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) with associated enteropathy
Chronic giardiasis in the setting of hypogammaglobulinemia
next_steps: Urgent surgical consultation for potential resection of ischemic/aneurysmal jejunal segments
Surgical pathology assessment of resected tissue with immunohistochemistry (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD20, CD30, CD56, TCR-beta/gamma)
Repeat immunoglobulin panel and lymphocyte subset analysis
Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate to evaluate for systemic lymphoproliferative disorder
Staging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and PET scan to evaluate for occult lymphoma
Repeat endoscopic evaluation/biopsy of the jejunal stricture if surgery is delayed
Transfusion of packed red blood cells for symptomatic anemia

differential_diagnoses: Intestinal T-cell lymphoma (likely monomorphic epitheliotropic intestinal T-cell lymphoma)
Common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) with chronic enteropathy
Chronic lymphoproliferative disorder of NK cells
Small bowel ischemia secondary to lymphoma-associated vasculopathy
next_steps: Urgent surgical consultation for resection of the symptomatic, ischemic, and aneurysmal jejunal segments
Surgical pathology assessment of resected tissue with comprehensive flow cytometry and T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies
Evaluation for occult systemic lymphoma including bone marrow biopsy and PET/CT
Serum free light chain assay to further characterize the monoclonal gammopathy
Evaluation by hematology/oncology to monitor the clonal NK-cell population
Maintenance of parenteral nutrition and optimization of nutritional status
Intravenous immunoglobulin replacement therapy to support immune function during diagnostic workup
